Person's Economic Standing Affects Credibility of Valuable Item Claims

micro_segments.title: Mishnah Case 1: Spark Flies from Blacksmith's Hammer into Public Domain
Mishnah Case 2: Camel Laden with Flax; Flax Enters Shop and Ignites Candle
Liability Assignment: Camel Owner Liable; Flax Intrudes into Private Domain
Reverse Case: Shop Owner Put Lamp Outside in Public Domain
Equivalence Principle: Bringing Fuel or Bringing Flame Both Create Liability

macro_segments.title: Chanukah Candle Exception and Requirements for Placement

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Rabbi Yehuda Exempts Chanukah Candle from Liability
Ravina and Rava: Chanukah Candle Must Be Within Ten Tefachim of Ground
Why Within Ten Tefachim: Special License to Perform Mitzvah Safely
Halacha Connection: Only Lowest Ten Tefachim Legally Considered Reshut Harabim
Why Not Higher: Rabbis Made Mitzvah Easy; Flaming Purpose Requires Visibility
Rav Kahana: Chanukah Candle Above Twenty Amah Disqualified Like Sukkah and Mavoi
Conceptual Parallel: Sukkah and Mavoi Both Have Twenty Amah Limit
Visibility Principle: Above Twenty Amah, Objects Not Recognizable from Below

macro_segments.title: Introduction to Perek Meruba: Theft vs. Robbery and Monetary Fines

micro_segments.title: New Topic: Distinction Between Kefel and Arba'ah Vachamisha Fines
Mishnah: Kefel More Extensive Than Arba'ah Vachamisha
Kefel Applies to Any Theft; Arba'ah Vachamisha Only for Ox and Sheep
Exceptions: Thief Stealing from Thief; Different Agents for Theft and Slaughter
Deriving from Pasuk: Stolen from Person's House, Not Thief's House

macro_segments.title: Expansion of Kefel Applicability: Toen Ta'anat Ganav

micro_segments.title: Question: Does Kefel Apply to Both Actual Thieves and Toen Ta'anat Ganav?
Definition: Toen Ta'anat Ganav - Shomer Falsely Claiming Theft
Gemara Resolution: Kefel Does Apply to Toen; Arba'ah Vachamisha Does Not
Tosafot Debate: Does Mishnah's Meruba Mean Complete List or Partial?
Ri Me'Orleans: Meruba Lists Examples, Not Exhaustive List

macro_segments.title: Biblical Hermeneutics: Klal U'Prat U'Klal on Sheomrim and Applicability Limits

micro_segments.title: Source: Baraita on Oath Laws for Sheomrim Using Klal U'Prat U'Klal
Hermeneutic Rule: Klal, Prat, Klal Limits General Law to Specific Cases
Application: Limited to Movable Property with Intrinsic Value
Exclusions: Land, Slaves, Documents, Hekdesh
Documents Have No Intrinsic Value; Burning Documents Is Garmi
Alternative Limit: Animals Whose Carcasses Transmit Impurity by Touch/Carrying
Rejected Proposal: Only Mammals, Not Birds; Baraita Lists Four Items
Resolution: Each Item Allows Its Own Klal U'Prat; Why Four Items Listed
Baraita Logic: Ox Alone Would Seem Limited; Donkey Alone Would Seem Limited
Sheep's Inclusion: Comes to Include Birds; Expands Applicability
